file_name: EMERALDHEALTHTHERAPEUTICSINC_06_10_2020-EX-4.5-CONSULTING AGREEMENT - DR. GAETANO MORELLO N.D. INC..txt

perturbation.original_text: The Contractor will devote sufficient time and attention to the business and affairs of Emerald to provide the Services, use his or her best efforts to promote the interests of Emerald, and will carry out his or her Services honestly, in good faith and in the best interests of Emerald.
perturbation.changed_text: The Contractor will devote some time and attention to the business and affairs of Emerald to provide the Services, use reasonable efforts to promote the interests of Emerald, and will carry out his or her Services with general regard to the interests of Emerald.
perturbation.explanation: This change weakens the Contractor's commitment. The original text requires 'sufficient time and attention' and 'best efforts,' implying a strong dedication. The modified text replaces these with 'some time and attention' and 'reasonable efforts,' reducing the required level of commitment. Additionally, changing 'in good faith and in the best interests of Emerald' to 'with general regard to the interests of Emerald' further diminishes the contractor's obligation. This contradicts section 2.2 which states that the Contractor's Services are described on Schedule 'B' on the terms and conditions of this agreement. Schedule B outlines specific tasks, implying a higher level of commitment than the modified text suggests.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Services. The Company engages the Contractor to fulfill the services (the "Services") as described on Schedule "B" on the terms and conditions of this Agreement. The Services may be replaced, amended, superseded, or supplemented from time to time by agreement between the Company and the Contractor.
perturbation.justification: The modified text weakens the contractor's commitment from 'sufficient time and attention' and 'best efforts' to 'some time and attention' and 'reasonable efforts'. The original text imposes a strong obligation for the contractor to act 'in good faith and in the best interests of Emerald.' The change modifies this to 'with general regard to the interests of Emerald.' This directly contradicts the 'Services' section which states that the contractor is engaged to 'fulfill the services...as described on Schedule 'B' on the terms and conditions of this Agreement', implying a specific and potentially more rigorous commitment than the amended 'general regard' suggests.

perturbation.original_text: Emerald shall not be obliged to deduct or retain from the Fees due to the Contractor, nor shall it be obliged to remit same to the required governmental authority, any amounts that may be required by law or regulation to be deducted, retained and remitted including, without limitation, Federal and Provincial or State Income Tax, Workers' Compensation and Pension Plan deductions and remittances. All such remittances and other payments are entirely the responsibility of the Contractor and the Contractor hereby indemnifies and saves Emerald and its Board members and officers harmless from any liability of any kind whatsoever that they may incur as a result of the Contractor's failure to make such remittances or payments.
perturbation.changed_text: Emerald may, at its discretion, deduct or retain from the Fees due to the Contractor, amounts required by law or regulation to be deducted, retained and remitted including, without limitation, Federal and Provincial or State Income Tax, Workers' Compensation and Pension Plan deductions and remittances. In the event that Emerald does deduct and remit such amounts, it shall provide documentation to the Contractor. Otherwise, all such remittances and other payments are the responsibility of the Contractor.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity about who is ultimately responsible for tax remittances. The original text clearly states the Contractor is solely responsible. The revised text says Emerald *may* deduct and remit, implying they might take on this responsibility, but if they don't, it defaults back to the Contractor. This contradicts section 3.1, Fee, in which states a fixed Fee amount. This raises concern because it's unsure whether the fee stated includes the taxes or not and who is responsible to pay that.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Fee. The Company will pay to the Contractor an annual fee (the "Fee") of $240,000. per year plus plus GST and applicable taxes, if any.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces ambiguity regarding responsibility for tax remittances. The original text clearly places this responsibility solely on the Contractor. The revised text allows Emerald to deduct and remit these amounts at its discretion, creating uncertainty. This contradicts the original section stating that the Company will pay the Contractor an annual fee (the "Fee") of $240,000 per year plus GST and applicable taxes, if any, in which it's unclear whether the $240,000 includes taxes and who is responsible for paying those taxes.

perturbation.original_text: The term of this Agreement shall commence on January 10th, 2019 and shall expire on the day that is twenty-four (24) months from that date (the "Term of Engagement") unless terminated earlier in accordance with this Agreement. The parties may mutually agree to extend this Agreement in writing and all terms and conditions hereof shall remain in effect during any extension unless the parties agree otherwise.
perturbation.changed_text: The term of this Agreement shall commence on January 10th, 2019 and shall automatically renew for successive twelve (12) month periods unless either party provides written notice of termination at least sixty (60) days prior to the end of the then-current term. The parties may still mutually agree to extend this Agreement in writing.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a new renewal clause that conflicts with the original fixed-term duration. The original text specifies a 24-month term, whereas the modification introduces automatic renewals unless notice is given. This new rule conflicts with section 5.2, Contractor's Right to Terminate Agreement for any Reason, because is not clear if the contractor is only able to terminate when the term of engagement is not active, due to the automatical renewal stated in this perturbation.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Contractor's Right to Terminate Agreement for any Reason. The Contractor may terminate this Agreement and his or her engagement for any reason at any time upon providing 30 days advance notice in writing to Emerald. Termination will be effective, at Emeralds' election, on a date which is no earlier than the date such notice is received and no later than the date which is 30 days following that date.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces an automatic renewal clause, fundamentally altering the contract's duration from a fixed 24-month term to a potentially indefinite term with 12-month renewals. This directly contradicts the contractor's right to terminate the agreement for any reason with 30 days' notice, as outlined in the contradicted text. The renewal clause creates uncertainty about when and how the contractor can exercise their termination right, especially if the agreement is automatically renewed. The original text allowed termination at any time with notice, but the renewal makes that right unclear. This falls under the 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' definition because it introduces inconsistent obligations.
